Ser
Harras Harlaw
the Knight
Harras Harlaw, by Ilich Henriquez © Fantasy Flight Games
Alias The Knight[1][2]
Titles
  • Ser[1]
  • Knight of Grey Garden[1]
  • Lord of Greyshield[3]
Allegiance House Harlaw of Grey Garden
Culture ironborn
Father Unknown Harlaw
Mother Unknown Serrett
Personal arms Quartered: a silver scythe on black, a peacock on cream
(Quarterly first and fourth, sable, a scythe argent, second and third, carnation, a peacock in his pride proper)
Books
  • A Feast for Crows (appears)
  • A Dance with Dragons (appendix)
  • The Winds of Winter (mentioned)

Harras Harlaw, known as the Knight, is the head of House Harlaw of Grey Garden and the knight of Grey Garden.[1][3] A knight, Harras is the heir of House Harlaw of Ten Towers, as its current lord, Rodrik Harlaw, has no surviving children.[4]

Contents

  • 1 Appearance
  • 2 History
  • 3 Recent Events
    • 3.1 A Feast for Crows
  • 4 Family
  • 5 References

Appearance[ ]

See also: Images of Harras Harlaw

Harras is tall with a long face and is austere. He bears a Valyrian steel sword, Nightfall.[2]

Harras's personal sigil is quartered: a silver scythe on black, a peacock on cream.[4][5] The scythe is from his Harlaw father and the peacock, the blazon of House Serrett, is from his mother.[4]

History[ ]

In his youth Harras was a good friend of Rodrik Greyjoy. Victarion Greyjoy enjoyed thrashing him when they were younger.[2]

Recent Events[ ]

A Feast for Crows[ ]

The banner of Ser Harras is spotted by Asha Greyjoy at Ten Towers.[4]

Harras is spotted by Aeron Greyjoy at the kingsmoot on Old Wyk. He is one of Asha's champions at the kingsmoot.[6]

Hotho Harlaw reports to Victarion Greyjoy that during the taking of the Shields, Harras planted his standard beneath Grimston, the Grimm castle on Greyshield, and fought and defeated seven men, slaying five in the process. The septon in the castle believed the gods had spoken and surrendered the castle to Harras. He is then named the new Lord of Greyshield by Euron Greyjoy, King of the Isles and the North. Hotho believes that this makes himself the new heir to Ten Towers.[2]
